cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So the question is: Today is 9/7 (which I assume is September 7th), and Jane is watching NFL 2003. The question is asking what the date was a month ago, in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given from A to F. First, I need to determine what date is exactly one month before September 7, 2003. But wait, sometimes when people say "a month ago," they might just subtract one month from the current month, keeping the same day. So if today is September 7, subtracting one month would take us to August 7. Let me check if that's correct. August has 31 days, so August 7 is a valid date. If that's the case, the answer would be August 7, 2003, which would be 08/07/2003. That's option F. But I need to check the options again to make sure. Looking at the options, option F is 08/07/2003. But let me make sure I'm not missing something. Sometimes when calculating dates, depending on the method, if you subtract a month from September 7, you get August 7. So that's straightforward. But I wonder if there's any trick here. The NFL 2003 part—does that affect the calculation? Maybe not directly. It just gives context for the year.
